Step Name: Load external market-cap snapshot
What It Does: Read the externally provided market-cap file (mkt_cap0224.csv, a one-date snapshot in Data/latest_data/). Standardise column names; exclude stocks whose short name contains 'B' or 'ST' (B-shares / ST-risk stocks); drop rows with missing/zero market value; convert to 亿元.
Reads From: Data/latest_data/mkt_cap0224.csv  (external, columns: tradeDate, ticker, secShortName, marketValue)

Step Name: Bin universe into 20 equal-count market-cap bins
What It Does: Sort all eligible stocks by market_cap_yi (亿元) ascending. Use pd.qcut with N_BINS=20 to assign each stock a bin_id (1=smallest ... 20=largest). Compute bin range labels in 亿元. Output CSV 1: full universe with bin assignment.
Saves To: Results/MarketCapBins/market_cap_bins.csv  (cols: tradedate, ticker, name, market_cap_yi, bin_id, bin_range_yi)

Step Name: Sample one stock per bin -> portfolio ticker list
What It Does: For each of the 20 bins, randomly sample one stock (RANDOM_SEED controls reproducibility). Output CSV 2: 20-stock portfolio, one per market-cap bin. The ticker column of this file is the recommended input for the downstream pipeline.

Step Name: Download daily price data via yfinance
What It Does: For each chosen ticker (taken from S0-03 or manually specified), download adjusted daily close prices from Yahoo Finance using yfinance (auto_adjust=True). Suffix: .SS for Shanghai (6xxxxx), .SZ for Shenzhen (0xxxxx/3xxxxx). Applies random sleep (6-9s) between requests to avoid rate-limiting. Saves one CSV per ticker with columns [date, close].
Reads From: tickers list hardcoded at top of data_yf.py  (edit before running)  |  date range: 2021-01-01 to 2026-03-12
Saves To: Data/latest_data/{TICKER}.csv  (cols: date, close;  one file per ticker)

Step Name: Load price data & compute log-returns
What It Does: Load raw price CSV from DATA_DIR, slice training window, compute log-returns, define S0 and annualised realised vol.
Saves To: used in memory; contributes to model.json / h_series.csv / params.csv

Step Name: Initial GARCH variance seed (h0)
What It Does: Uses sample variance of training returns as initial variance seed for the MLE optimiser.

Step Name: MLE estimation of GARCH+Jump parameters
What It Does: Estimate omega, alpha, beta, lambda, mu_J, sigma_J via SLSQP with stationarity constraint (alpha+beta<1).

Step Name: Refilter conditional variance to h_series
What It Does: Recompute full h_t series using final fitted parameters over the extended price window (incl. backtest period).

Step Name: Adaptive N-calibration
What It Does: Find required MC path count so eSSVI surface pricing error <= 50 bps; round up to N_block. Controls quality/speed trade-off for the full backtest.

Step Name: Generate random-path block
What It Does: Build pre-allocated Z (normal) + Poisson jump random matrices for full backtest horizon + max tenor. Re-used every day to avoid re-drawing.

Step Name: eSSVI vol-surface snapshot (optional)
What It Does: On PRINT_VOL_SURFACE_DATE: simulate one-date MC surface, invert BS prices to IV per (T,K), fit eSSVI smile per tenor, save formatted IV surface + PNG chart to Results/Charts/.

Step Name: Dynamic delta-hedging backtest
What It Does: Run trading loop over entry dates x tenors x q-grid. Each day: update h_t via GARCH recursion, simulate N_BACKTEST paths, price options, compute BS delta, hedge PnL. Full MC + eSSVI runs every day.
Reads From: price_df_full, h_series, garch/jump params, rand_block, strategy config

Step Name: Aggregate HE per (tenor, quantile) bucket
What It Does: For each (T_days, q) node in trades.csv, compute: n, k_mean, premium_mean, HE_mean, HE_std, HE_per_S_mean, HE_per_S_std, HE_per_S_tstat, HE_per_S_pval (one-sample t-test H0: E[HE/S]=0).
Saves To: he_agg DataFrame in memory (MultiIndex T_days x q)

Step Name: Build wide he_stats table + long he_detail
What It Does: Pivot he_agg into wide format (tenor_days | metric | q05...ATM...q95). Metrics: model_vol%, HE_per_S_mean_%, HE_per_S_std_%, HE_per_S_tstat, HE_per_S_pval. Also save long-format he_detail.csv for Stage 4 bias correction and Stage 5 cross-section. P-value < 0.05 indicates statistically significant mean HE bias.

Step Name: MC repricing with jump-param adjustments (ask & bid)
What It Does: For each quote side (ask/bid): adjust base jump params by config multipliers (S4_ASK/BID_LAM_MULT, MU_J_ADD, SIGMA_J_MULT). Generate S4_N_PATHS paths from (S_t, h_t). Price options at the FAIR K-grid from Stage 2. Invert MC prices to raw IV; fit eSSVI to smooth. Step-1 spread = p_ask_mc - p_bid_mc (pure jump-param effect).
Saves To: iv_ask_mc, iv_bid_mc, p_ask_mc, p_bid_mc  in memory

Step Name: HE bias correction -- shift mid vol using mu_HE
What It Does: Correct systematic model mispricing from Stage 3. Per (T,q): delta_sigma_bias = clip(-S4_HE_BIAS_COEF x mu_HE / vega_eff, +/-S4_HE_BIAS_CLIP). Shift mid vol AND both ask_mc/bid_mc by the same delta (preserves step-1 spread). mu_HE>0 (overpriced) -> lower mid; mu_HE<0 (underpriced) -> raise mid.
Reads From: iv_surface_df (Stage 2 model IV) + he_detail (Stage 3 mu_HE) + k_surface_df
Saves To: iv_mid_adj, iv_ask_mc_adj, iv_bid_mc_adj  in memory (bias-corrected surfaces)

Step Name: HE vol-spread overlay (sigma_HE only) + OTM floor
What It Does: mu_HE is NOT used here (fully absorbed by S4-10b). Per node: vega_eff = sqrt[(phi(d1)*sqrt(T))^2 + eps(T)^2] where eps = S4_VEGA_REG_ALPHA*0.4*sqrt(T). delta_sigma_ask = max(S4_ASK_SIGMA_COEF*sigma_HE/vega_eff, 0). OTM floor: delta_sigma_floor = S4_SPREAD_FLOOR_COEF*eps/vega_eff (widens spread at high-strike wings). iv_ask_final = iv_ask_mc_adj + delta_ask + floor; iv_bid_final = iv_bid_mc_adj - delta_bid - floor (floored at P_BID_MIN).
Reads From: iv_ask_mc_adj, iv_bid_mc_adj, iv_mid_adj (from S4-10b) + he_detail (sigma_HE) + k_surface_df

Step Name: Butterfly arb check + fix (Method B)
What It Does: Per tenor, sort strikes K ascending. For each interior triplet (K1<K2<K3): check p(K1)+p(K3) >= 2*p(K2). Fix: p(K2) <- (p(K1)+p(K3))/2 - eps. Iterate up to ARB_FIX_MAX_ITERS passes. Applied independently to p_ask and p_bid.
Saves To: {TICKER}_{STRAT}_price_surface.csv (MultiIndex tenor_days x metric; metrics: K_over_S, p_fair, p_fair_adj, p_ask_mc, p_bid_mc, p_ask_mc_adj, p_bid_mc_adj, p_ask, p_bid, spread, s1_spread, s1b_spread, s2_spread, iv_model, iv_model_adj, iv_ask_mc, iv_bid_mc, iv_ask, iv_bid)

Step Name: Test A -- std(HE/S) vs moneyness
What It Does: For each tenor T, compute std(HE/S) at each quantile sorted by log-moneyness k. Theory: std peaks at ATM (Gamma proportional to N'(d1) maximised there), decreasing toward wings.

Step Name: Test A2 -- mean(HE/S) vs moneyness (seller bias)
What It Does: For each tenor T, compute mean(HE/S) at each quantile. HE>0 -> seller profitable. Identifies systematic directional bias per (T,k) node.

Step Name: Test B -- std(HE/S) vs tenor (jump accumulation)
What It Does: For each quantile label, fit std^2(T) = C1 + C2*T via OLS. C2>0 confirms jump-component accumulation with tenor.

Step Name: Test C -- Cross-ticker HE bias heatmap
What It Does: Load he_detail for all CROSS_TICKERS. Per (T,q): cross_mean_%, cross_std_%, t-stat. |t|>1.96 -> significantly biased node (++ or --). Outputs mean heatmap, t-stat heatmap (source for Chart-3), sign map.

Step Name: Test D -- Spearman(HE std, market-cap rank) heatmap
What It Does: CROSS_TICKERS ordered small->large cap. Per (T,q): Spearman rho between mktcap rank and HE_per_S_std. rho<0 -> larger cap -> lower HE std. Source for Chart-4.

Step Name: Test E -- Average HE std by market-cap quintile
What It Does: Divide CROSS_TICKERS into 4 quintiles (Q1=smallest...Q4=largest). Per (T,q): mean HE_per_S_std per quintile. ATM-only staircase table + (Q1-Q4)/Q1 decline heatmap.

Step Name: Cross-stock HE mean t-stat heatmap
What It Does: Heatmap of per-(T,q) t-statistics (from S5-17) with significance stars. Skipped if file already exists (expensive cross-stock aggregate).

Step Name: Spearman rho heatmap (HE std vs market-cap rank)
What It Does: Heatmap of per-(T,q) Spearman rho values (from S5-18) with * for p<0.10. Skipped if file already exists.

Step Name: eSSVI 3D vol surface (per ticker)
What It Does: 3D surface plot of eSSVI implied vol across (tenor, moneyness). Always regenerated. Loops over all tickers in config.TICKERS.

Step Name: Vol smile bid-ask + price spread (per ticker)
What It Does: Two-panel chart for T=63d: Ask IV / Bid IV / Mid IV (bias-corrected iv_model_adj as main line) / Model IV reference (dotted). Right panel: price spread (p_ask-p_bid)/S. Always regenerated. Loops over all tickers in config.TICKERS.
